title: SkillMeta
description: Machine-readable sibling of SKILL.md. Locked contract once Module 3 (Runner) ships; additionalProperties is deliberately false to catch key typos early.

slug.description: URL-and-filesystem-safe identifier, 3-40 chars, starts with a letter.

trajectory_id.description: UUID of the source trajectory this skill was synthesized from.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the skill was written to disk.

name.description: Parameter identifier used in {name} references inside SKILL.md.

destructive_steps.description: 1-based indices of SKILL.md steps that require confirmation before execution.

steps.description: Optional per-step execution metadata. Each entry's number aligns with a 1-based step in SKILL.md's ## Steps section. Omitted entries (or an entirely absent steps array) tell the runner to fall through to computer-use for every step. Order independent: the runner indexes by step number.

intent.description: Short verb-phrase summary of what the step accomplishes (e.g. 'send_email', 'create_event', 'set_status'). Used by the runner to pick a hint when several apply.

execution_hints.description: Ordered execution candidates from most-preferred to least-preferred. The runner picks the first whose preconditions are satisfiable (MCP connected, browser focused, etc.).

executionHint.description: A single execution candidate for one workflow step. The shape varies by tier; each tier-specific field is optional but the runner enforces presence at execution time.

function.description: tier=mcp: function name within the named server.

arguments.description: tier=mcp: argument map. Values may contain {param} substitutions matching declared parameters.

url_pattern.description: tier=browser_dom: URL the runner should navigate to (or verify) before acting. May contain {param} substitutions.

selector.description: tier=browser_dom: CSS selector or aria-label for the element to interact with.

value.description: tier=browser_dom: literal text or URL for the action (e.g. text to type, URL to navigate to). May contain {param} substitutions.

summary.description: tier=computer_use: human/agent-readable description for pixel-grounded fallback.
